standards.description: Integrate evidence from multiple relevant historical sources and interpretations into a reasoned argument.

content.objective: Analyze a historical claim and select evidence that supports it.
content.source_context: Adapted classroom excerpt based on arguments for ratifying the U.S. Constitution.
passage: A large republic can make it more difficult for one faction to control the whole government.
Representatives chosen by the people may refine public views and protect the common good.
The proposed Constitution divides power so ambition may check ambition.
content.evidence_task: Select the sentence that best supports the claim that divided power helps prevent abuse.

study_mode.prompt: Read the source context, then identify the main claim and the evidence that supports it.

practice_mode.prompt: Write one sentence explaining how the evidence connects to the claim.

checks.question: Which sentence best supports the claim that divided power can limit abuse?

choices.text: A large republic can make it more difficult for one faction to control the whole government.
Representatives chosen by the people may refine public views and protect the common good.
The proposed Constitution divides power so ambition may check ambition.

feedback.correct: Yes. That sentence directly connects divided power with checking ambition.
feedback.incorrect: Look for the evidence that explicitly mentions divided power and checking ambition.

reflection_prompt.prompt: Explain why a historian should connect evidence back to a specific claim.
